plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-353 RP11.P05.M06.S08-RP11.P06.M04.S05 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-353 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP11.P05 fixture phase (M06 synthetic fixture tail, M07 test/golden, M08 Hermes evidence, M09 Claude review 20-row cycles, M10 closeout 8-row head) and opens the RP11.P06 permission foundation (M00-M04: permission matrix rows, view/search/mutation/export-download/share/AI-retrieval decision bindings, audit hint fields, matched rule capture, deny-over-allow checks, legal hold/ethical wall/object ACL interactions, review/approval-required routes, security trimming proofs, audit event expectations, permission fixtures, allowed/denied/cross-tenant/leak-prevention tests) across ten sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H11), Claude review packet (C11), and CP00-354 handoff, without opening runtime permission evaluation or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-353 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (150 units, risk C, RP11.P05.M06.S08-RP11.P06.M04.S05) and its production_ready claim is appropriately conservative and fully supported. The pack does NOT overclaim: command-evidence and manifest both record production_ready=false with status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout, and Hermes/local validation only report production_ready_candidate=true. Independent read-only inspection confirms: (1) the live closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-353 entry (ledger sha 13c7ed65..., deviation_from_plan=false) matches the registry binding, contract current_pack, and review baseline exactly; (2) registry CP353_REQUIREMENTS deliverable/phase/micro-title counts reconcile to 150 and match the prompt distribution; (3) all seven required public exports exist in service.js/validators.js and are re-exported via index.js, with freezeCp353Result forcing 30+ no-write/no-runtime flags to false; (4) model.test.js and the validator script load the live plan+contract, deepEqual the contract against registry constants, and assert coverage.valid, foundation.valid, deny-over-allow, and claude_is_final_approval=false; (5) command-evidence.json records all 23 build/test/validate commands exiting 0 including git diff --check. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold at every layer: no writeFileSync/exec/fetch/http in any changed source, contract+manifest no-write attestations all-false on dangerous flags, synthetic_only/no_real_data true. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (read_only=true, tools Read/Grep/Glob, claude_not_final_approver=true, human_final_approval_required) and is not final approval authority. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ review hardening is enforced via forbidden_review_evidence (auth failures, zero-byte/malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims) and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. One non-blocking P3 bookkeeping discrepancy noted in the generated changed-file-scope. No P0/P1/P2 issues.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists this pack's own cp00-353/ artifacts under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: In changed-file-scope.json, docs/closeout-packs/cp00-353/ (and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md) appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 138-139). The cp00-353/ directory holds this pack's own manifest.json and command-evidence.json, so labeling it 'unrelated' is inconsistent with the manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest line 6016 onward), which correctly excludes cp00-353/ and lists only the genuinely unrelated workbook/docx/ldip files. The discrepancy is purely a generated-bookkeeping labeling difference and has no effect on review integrity: active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty in both changed-file-scope.json (line 134) and validation-report.json (line 24), so nothing in active pack scope was improperly omitted, and the no-write/no-real-data boundaries are unaffected.
findings.recommendation: Optionally align the changed-file-scope generator so the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack-id>/ artifacts are not also categorized as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, matching the manifest's cleaner classification. No code or boundary change required; safe to close out as-is.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; does not block pack closeout or goal closeout. Recommend tracking as a minor generator-hygiene cleanup.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-353 time expense core p05 closeout p06 permission foundation descriptor pack
